name: Weaviate
what_it_does: Open-source vector database with built-in vectorization modules, hybrid search (BM25 + vector), and a GraphQL/REST/gRPC API for building semantic search and RAG applications.
use_cases: RAG pipelines with built-in embedding generation via module integrations (OpenAI, Cohere, etc.)
Hybrid search combining keyword BM25 and semantic vector search
Multi-modal search over text, images, and audio in a single index
Knowledge graph construction with object cross-references
Semantic document retrieval with fine-grained filtering
not_for: Simple key-value lookups or relational queries
Teams who want fully managed infrastructure without any DevOps overhead (self-hosted Weaviate requires operational effort)
Workloads requiring exact keyword matching only
best_when: You want an open-source vector database with powerful hybrid search, built-in vectorization, and the option to self-host or use managed cloud.
avoid_when: You need a zero-ops managed vector store with minimal configuration or are already invested in a Pinecone/Qdrant ecosystem.

auth.notes: API key auth supported on Weaviate Cloud Services (WCS). OIDC/OAuth2 for enterprise. Self-hosted can run with no auth (dev mode) or with full RBAC. WCS provides admin and read-only keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Weaviate Cloud free sandbox (14-day expiry); self-hosted is free indefinitely
pricing.paid_tiers: WCS Standard from ~$25/month; Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: Open source (BSD-3). Self-hosting is free. Weaviate Cloud Services (WCS) is the managed offering with paid tiers. Sandboxes expire after 14 days.

performance.rate_limits: No hard rate limits on self-hosted; WCS limits vary by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors return structured error arrays. REST errors include status and message. gRPC errors follow standard codes. Error messages are useful but occasionally require schema knowledge to interpret.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Objects identified by deterministic UUID (can be specified or auto-generated). Upserting with a known UUID is idempotent — updates in place rather than duplicating.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL API for queries, REST for CRUD — agents need to handle two different interfaces
Schema must be defined before data insertion — dynamic schema is available but types must be correct
Built-in vectorizer modules require API keys to be set at startup (e.g., OPENAI_APIKEY env var)
WCS sandboxes expire after 14 days — all data is lost; use persistent clusters for production
Cross-references (object relationships) require separate API calls to resolve — adds latency for linked data
gRPC is now the recommended high-performance API but requires the newer v4 Python client
